title: Reentrancy — Checks-Effects-Interactions Pattern Verification
summary: Verify that every external call site in a Solidity contract follows Checks-Effects-Interactions order, preventing reentrancy attacks by ensuring state is finalized before control leaves the contract.
preconditions: Contract contains at least one external call site: .call(), .transfer(), .send(), or an interface/library call that invokes external code.

cost_estimate.notes: Slither covers items 1-3 partially. Items 4 (cross-function reentrancy) and 5 (mock attacker) require manual analysis and test authoring respectively.

checklist.item: Map every external call site in the contract: .call(), .transfer(), .send(), and any interface or library call that invokes external code.
checklist.rationale: You cannot audit what you have not located. A complete call-site map is the prerequisite for all subsequent checks.

checklist.item: At each call site, verify CEI order: all state variable writes complete before the external call executes.
checklist.rationale: An attacker re-entering the function before state is updated can drain funds or corrupt protocol state. CEI violation is the root cause of the DAO hack and most reentrancy losses.

tooling.manual: For each entry in external_call_inventory, trace all preceding state writes and confirm no write occurs after the call.

checklist.item: Confirm the nonReentrant modifier (or equivalent mutex) is present on all public and external state-changing functions that make external calls.
checklist.rationale: CEI order alone does not protect against cross-function reentrancy. The mutex prevents re-entry through any guarded function sharing the same lock.

tooling.automated: grep -rn 'nonReentrant\|ReentrancyGuard' src/ — verify coverage against external_call_inventory
tooling.manual: Cross-reference each function in external_call_inventory against the modifier list.

checklist.item: Check for cross-function reentrancy: identify pairs of functions that share state where one makes an external call and the other reads or writes that state.
checklist.rationale: Single-function reentrancy guards do not protect against an attacker re-entering via a different function that shares the same storage slot.

tooling.automated: slither --detect reentrancy-no-eth (partial — catches benign cross-function cases)
tooling.manual: Build a shared-state dependency graph across all functions; flag any function pair where one has an external call and the other accesses the same slot.

checklist.item: Test with a mock attacker contract that calls back into the target on receive() or fallback() and attempts to re-enter the function under test.
checklist.rationale: Static analysis confirms structure; dynamic testing confirms behavior under adversarial conditions. A passing mock attacker test is the only way to validate that guards hold end-to-end.

tooling.automated: forge test --match-contract MockAttacker (Foundry) or hardhat test --grep reentrancy
tooling.manual: Author MockAttacker.sol: implement receive() that calls back the target function; assert attacker balance does not increase.

successConditions: external_call_inventory complete and cross-referenced against source
No CEI violation found at any call site in cei_order_verification
nonReentrant modifier coverage == 1.0 across all guarded entry points
Mock attacker test produces zero successful re-entries
failureConditions: Any external call executes before all relevant state writes in the same function — CRITICAL
nonReentrant modifier absent on a public state-changing function that makes an external call — HIGH
Cross-function reentrancy path identified with no mutex protection — HIGH
Mock attacker test succeeds in re-entering the function — CRITICAL

inputs.name: contract_source
inputs.description: Solidity source files (.sol) for the contract under audit

inputs.name: test_environment
inputs.description: Hardhat or Foundry environment for dynamic attacker simulation

outputs.name: reentrancy_clearance
outputs.description: True when all 5 checks pass with zero violations

outputs.name: external_call_inventory
outputs.description: Map of all external call sites annotated with CEI status and mutex coverage
